tasks.title: Implement Internationalization (i18n) with Paraglide-js
tasks.description: Set up paraglide-js to enable multilingual support for the Deskohub website, allowing content to be displayed in both Czech and English with a foundation for adding more languages in the future.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify installation and configuration:
   - Confirm paraglide-js packages are correctly installed
   - Validate the configuration file has proper settings
   - Check that translation files are in the correct format and location

2. Test language switching functionality:
   - Manually switch between Czech and English using the language switcher
   - Verify that the UI updates correctly when language is changed
   - Confirm that language preference is persisted between page reloads

3. Validate translations:
   - Create a checklist of all UI elements that should be translated
   - Systematically check each page in both languages to ensure all text is properly translated
   - Verify that no hardcoded text remains in the components

4. Test language detection:
   - Test with different browser language settings to verify automatic language detection
   - If using path-based routing, verify that URLs with language prefixes load the correct language

5. Responsive testing:
   - Ensure language switcher works correctly on mobile devices
   - Verify that translated text displays properly across different screen sizes

6. Performance testing:
   - Measure initial load time with and without internationalization
   - Verify that language switching is performant and doesn't cause noticeable delays

7. Accessibility testing:
   - Ensure language attributes are correctly set for screen readers
   - Verify that language switcher is keyboard accessible

8. Create automated tests:
   - Write unit tests for language switching functionality
   - Create integration tests that verify key components display correct text in each language

tasks.title: Create Reservation Form Component with Form Validation
tasks.description: Develop a comprehensive reservation form component for the Deskohub website that captures user information, reservation details, and special requirements with proper validation using shadcn/ui and React Hook Form.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit testing:
   - Test form validation logic:
     - Verify required fields trigger appropriate validation errors when empty
     - Test email format validation with valid and invalid email addresses
     - Test phone number format validation with various formats
     - Verify date validation prevents selecting dates in the past
   - Test form submission handling:
     - Mock API calls and verify proper data formatting
     - Test loading states during submission
     - Verify error handling when submission fails

2. Integration testing:
   - Test the form within the page context to ensure it renders correctly
   - Verify form interactions with other components (if any)
   - Test that the form properly uses the theme context for styling
   - Verify internationalization works correctly for all form elements

3. Accessibility testing:
   - Verify all form fields have proper labels and ARIA attributes
   - Test keyboard navigation through the entire form
   - Ensure screen readers can properly announce form fields and validation errors
   - Test with various color contrast settings to ensure readability

4. Manual testing:
   - Complete end-to-end form submission with valid data
   - Test form on different screen sizes to verify responsive behavior
   - Verify form appearance in both light and dark modes
   - Test all validation error messages by deliberately entering invalid data
   - Verify date and time pickers work correctly
   - Test with different browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari) to ensure cross-browser compatibility

5. User acceptance testing:
   - Have team members complete the form to identify any usability issues
   - Verify the form matches the design specifications
   - Ensure the form integrates well with the overall website design and user flow

tasks.title: Implement URL-based Language Switching with Next.js App Router

tasks.title: Implement CSS Grid with Named Areas for Booking Form
tasks.description: Create a semantic, responsive grid layout for the booking form using CSS Grid with named areas, ensuring proper display on both desktop and mobile devices without relying on problematic Tailwind plugins.
tasks.details:
## 🎯 Objective Implement CSS Grid with named areas for the booking form to create a semantic, responsive layout where: - **Desktop**: Date/Time and Contact sections display side-by-side - **Mobile**: All sections stack vertically - **Goal**: Eliminate wrapper divs and create cleaner, more maintainable code ## 📍 Current State - **File**: `features/booking/components/booking-form.tsx` - **Current Layout**: Uses standard Tailwind responsive grid (`md:grid-cols-2`) - **Working Features**: Form functions correctly, `Intl.ListFormat` internationalization implemented - **Status**: Grid template areas implementation **NOT committed** (left in working directory) ## 🔧 Implementation Steps 1. Create custom CSS classes in `globals.css` using Tailwind's @layer components: ```css @layer components { .booking-form-grid { display: grid; gap: 1.5rem; grid-template-columns: 1fr; grid-template-areas: "datetime-info" "contact-info" "table-preferences" "special-requests" "pricing" "submit"; } @media (min-width: 768px) { .booking-form-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; grid-template-areas: "datetime-info contact-info" "table-preferences table-preferences" "special-requests special-requests" "pricing pricing" "submit submit"; } } .grid-area-datetime-info { grid-area: datetime-info; } .grid-area-contact-info { grid-area: contact-info; } .grid-area-table-preferences { grid-area: table-preferences; } .grid-area-special-requests { grid-area: special-requests; } .grid-area-pricing { grid-area: pricing; } .grid-area-submit { grid-area: submit; } } ``` 2. Update the booking form component to use the new grid layout: ```tsx // features/booking/components/booking-form.tsx export function BookingForm() { // Existing form implementation... return (
{/* Date and time selection fields */} {/* Contact information fields */} {/* Table preferences section */} {/* Special requests textarea */} {/* Pricing information */}
{/* Submit button */}
); } ``` 3. Remove any existing grid-related Tailwind classes that might conflict with the new CSS Grid implementation: - Remove `md:grid-cols-2` and similar classes - Remove any attempted grid area implementations using plugins - Keep other utility classes for spacing, colors, etc. 4. Add comments explaining the grid area implementation for future maintainability: ```tsx // CSS Grid with named areas is implemented via custom classes in globals.css // This approach avoids issues with Tailwind grid area plugins // See @layer components in globals.css for the grid definitions ``` ## 🚨 Issues Encountered and Avoided ### 1. **First Plugin Attempt: `tailwindcss-grid-areas`** **Problem**: Plugin not generating CSS styles ```bash bun add -D tailwindcss-grid-areas ``` **Configuration Attempted**: ```typescript // tailwind.config.ts plugins: [require("tailwindcss-animate"), require("tailwindcss-grid-areas")] ``` **Syntax Used**: ```tsx className="grid-areas-[datetime-info,contact-info,table-preferences,special-requests,pricing,submit]" ``` **Issue**: - Classes appeared in HTML but no corresponding CSS generated - `grep -i "grid-areas" .next/static/css/...` returned no results - Plugin appears to be non-functional or misconfigured ### 2. **Second Plugin Attempt: `@savvywombat/tailwindcss-grid-areas`** **Problem**: Different syntax requirements, configuration complexity **Configuration**: ```typescript // tailwind.config.ts theme: { extend: { gridTemplateAreas: { 'booking-form': [ 'datetime-info contact-info', 'table-preferences table-preferences', 'special-requests special-requests', 'pricing pricing', 'submit submit', ], 'booking-form-mobile': [ 'datetime-info', 'contact-info', 'table-preferences', 'special-requests', 'pricing', 'submit', ], }, }, }, plugins: [require("@savvywombat/tailwindcss-grid-areas")] ``` **Syntax Used**: ```tsx className="grid-areas-booking-form-mobile md:grid-areas-booking-form" // Children: className="grid-in-datetime-info" ``` **Issue**: - Generated CSS in debug mode but not in production build - Plugin seems to work in isolation but fails in Next.js build process - Complex configuration required for each grid layout ### 3. **Plugin-less Approach with Arbitrary Values** **Problem**: Tailwind CSS limitations with complex arbitrary values **Attempted Implementation**: ```tsx className="grid gap-6 grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 [grid-template-areas:'datetime-info''contact-info''table-preferences''special-requests''pricing''submit'] md:[grid-template-areas:'datetime-info_contact-info''table-preferences_table-preferences''special-requests_special-requests''pricing_pricing''submit_submit']" ``` **Issue**: - Extremely long class names (unmaintainable) - Unclear if Tailwind supports complex grid-template-areas in arbitrary values - Potential parsing issues with multi-line grid template areas ## 🛠️ Debugging Steps Taken 1. **CSS Generation Testing**: ```bash bunx tailwindcss -i ./app/globals.css -o ./debug.css --content="./features/booking/components/booking-form.tsx" grep -A 5 -B 5 "grid-areas" ./debug.css ``` 2. **Build Analysis**: ```bash find .next -name "*.css" | head -5 grep -i "grid" .next/static/css/app/\[locale\]/layout.css ``` 3. **Plugin Verification**: - Confirmed plugin installation in `package.json` - Verified plugin configuration in `tailwind.config.ts` - Tested CSS generation in isolation ## 🔍 Technical Context - **Tailwind CSS Version**: 3.4.17 - **Next.js Version**: 15.3.5 - **Build Tool**: Bun - **Issue Pattern**: Plugins work in development but fail in production builds - **Tailwind Arbitrary Values**: May not support complex grid-template-areas syntax
tasks.testStrategy:
## 🧪 Test Strategy

1. **Visual testing on multiple devices and screen sizes**:
   - Test on mobile devices (iPhone SE, iPhone 12, Galaxy S21)
   - Test on tablets (iPad, iPad Pro)
   - Test on desktop monitors (1080p, 1440p, 4K)
   - Verify the layout correctly switches between stacked (mobile) and side-by-side (desktop) at the md breakpoint

2. **Responsive breakpoint testing**:
   - Use browser developer tools to resize the viewport and observe layout changes
   - Verify the transition between layouts is smooth and occurs at 768px as defined
   - Check that no content overflows or becomes misaligned during transition

3. **Cross-browser compatibility testing**:
   - Test in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge
   - Verify grid areas render correctly in all browsers
   - Check for any browser-specific rendering issues

4. **Form functionality preservation**:
   - Complete and submit the form to ensure all functionality remains intact
   - Verify form validation still works properly
   - Confirm all form fields are accessible and can be filled out correctly

5. **Performance impact assessment**:
   - Compare page load times before and after implementation
   - Check for any layout shifts during page load (CLS)
   - Verify the CSS bundle size impact is minimal

6. **Accessibility testing**:
   - Test keyboard navigation through the form
   - Verify screen reader compatibility with the new layout
   - Check that focus order follows a logical sequence in both layouts

7. **Code review checklist**:
   - Confirm no Tailwind plugins are being used for grid areas
   - Verify CSS is properly organized in the @layer components section
   - Check that all grid areas are correctly named and applied
   - Ensure the implementation is maintainable and well-documented

8. **Specific test cases**:
   - **Mobile Layout**: All sections stack vertically
   - **Desktop Layout**: Date/Time and Contact sections side-by-side
   - **Responsive Transition**: Smooth layout changes at breakpoints
   - **Form Functionality**: All form interactions work correctly
   - **Cross-browser**: Test in Chrome, Firefox, Safari

tasks.title: Implement Safe Area Insets for Device Screen Cutouts

tasks.testStrategy:
## Test Strategy

1. **Device Testing Matrix**:
   - Test on iOS devices with notches (iPhone X and newer)
   - Test on iOS devices with Dynamic Island (iPhone 14 Pro and newer)

   - Test on devices with rounded corners
   - Test on standard devices without special cutouts (as control)

2. **Orientation Testing**:
   - Test in portrait mode
   - Test in landscape mode (both left and right orientations)
   - Verify insets adjust correctly when rotating the device

3. **Component-Specific Testing**:
   - **Header/Navigation**: Verify content doesn't overlap with notches or status bar
   - **Mobile Menu**: Check that the slide-out menu respects safe areas
   - **Fixed Position Elements**: Ensure bottom navigation bars, FABs, and other fixed elements respect safe areas
   - **Full-Screen Modals**: Test that modals and dialogs extend properly but keep content within safe areas
   - **Gallery/Lightbox**: Verify full-screen images respect safe areas
   - **Forms**: Check that input fields and buttons near screen edges are fully accessible

4. **Visual Regression Testing**:
   - Take screenshots before and after implementation on various devices
   - Compare to ensure no unintended layout changes occurred
   - Verify UI elements maintain proper spacing from screen edges

5. **Debug Mode Testing**:
   - Verify the debug visualization correctly shows safe area insets
   - Confirm the keyboard shortcut (Alt+Shift+D) correctly toggles debug mode
   - Check that debug visualization accurately reflects actual insets

6. **Browser Compatibility**:
   - Test in Safari on iOS (primary target)
   - Test in Chrome on Android
   - Test in other mobile browsers (Firefox, Samsung Internet)
   - Verify fallback behavior works correctly on unsupported browsers

7. **Performance Testing**:
   - Measure any impact on rendering performance
   - Check for layout shifts when insets are applied
   - Verify smooth transitions when device orientation changes

8. **Accessibility Testing**:
   - Verify all interactive elements remain fully accessible
   - Check that touch targets near screen edges are not compromised
   - Ensure no content is hidden or difficult to access due to insets

9. **Manual Testing Checklist**:
   - [ ] All fixed headers respect top safe area inset
   - [ ] All fixed footers respect bottom safe area inset
   - [ ] Side menus respect left/right safe areas
   - [ ] Full-width containers properly handle horizontal safe areas
   - [ ] No content is cut off by notches or rounded corners
   - [ ] No interactive elements are positioned under notches or rounded corners
   - [ ] Layout adjusts properly when rotating device

tasks.title: Audit and Remove Unused Components
tasks.description: Systematically audit all components in the codebase and delete any that are not being used anywhere, including unused imports, unreferenced components, and dead code to improve maintainability and performance.
tasks.details:
## Implementation Steps

1. **Set Up Static Analysis Tools**:
   ```bash
   # Install ESLint plugins for detecting unused code
   npm install --save-dev eslint-plugin-unused-imports eslint-plugin-no-unused-components
   ```

   Update ESLint configuration:
   ```javascript
   // .eslintrc.js
   module.exports = {
     // ... existing config
     plugins: [
       // ... existing plugins
       'unused-imports',
       'no-unused-components'
     ],
     rules: {
       // ... existing rules
       'unused-imports/no-unused-imports': 'error',
       'unused-imports/no-unused-vars': 'warn',
       'no-unused-components/no-unused-components': 'warn'
     }
   }
   ```

2. **Create an Inventory of All Components**:
   ```bash
   # Find all component files in the project
   find ./components -type f -name "*.tsx" > component-inventory.txt
   find ./app -type f -name "*.tsx" >> component-inventory.txt
   ```

3. **Run Static Analysis**:
   ```bash
   # Run ESLint to identify unused imports and components
   npx eslint --ext .ts,.tsx . --report-unused-disable-directives
   
   # Use next-unused to find unused files and exports
   npx next-unused
   ```

4. **Create a Tracking Spreadsheet**:
   - Component name
   - File path
   - Current usage status (Used/Unused/Uncertain)
   - Verification method
   - Notes
   - Action (Keep/Remove)

5. **Manual Verification Process**:
   - For each component flagged as potentially unused:
     - Search codebase for direct imports: `grep -r "import.*ComponentName" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" .`
     - Search for dynamic imports: `grep -r "dynamic.*ComponentName" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" .`
     - Search for string references (for dynamically loaded components): `grep -r "['|\"]ComponentName['|\"]" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" .`
     - Check for components used only in specific environments (dev/prod)

6. **Create a Backup Branch**:
   ```bash
   git checkout -b component-cleanup-backup
   git add .
   git commit -m "Backup before component cleanup"
   git checkout -b component-cleanup
   ```

7. **Removal Process**:
   - Start with clearly unused components first
   - Remove one component at a time
   - Run the application and tests after each removal
   - Document each removal in the tracking spreadsheet

8. **Handle Edge Cases**:
   - Components used only in specific environments
   - Components imported but not directly referenced
   - Components used via dynamic imports
   - Components referenced by string name

9. **Update Import Statements**:
   - Remove unused imports from files
   - Clean up any import statements that reference removed components

10. **Final Verification**:
    - Run full test suite
    - Manually test key application flows
    - Verify build process completes without errors
    - Check bundle size before and after to quantify improvements

11. **Documentation Update**:
    - Update component documentation to remove references to deleted components
    - Document the cleanup process and results
    - Create a summary report of removed components and impact on bundle size
tasks.testStrategy:
## Test Strategy

1. **Pre-Cleanup Baseline**:
   - Create a full test suite run report before starting
   - Measure and document current bundle size
   - Document current build time
   - Create a visual snapshot of key pages for comparison

2. **Incremental Testing**:
   ```bash
   # After each component removal
   npm run test
   npm run build
   npm run lint
   ```

3. **Regression Testing**:
   - Create a checklist of key application features
   - Manually verify each feature after component removal
   - Test all routes to ensure pages load correctly
   - Test all interactive elements to ensure functionality is preserved

4. **Visual Regression Testing**:
   - Compare before/after screenshots of key pages
   - Verify no unexpected visual changes occurred
   - Test on multiple screen sizes (mobile, tablet, desktop)

5. **Performance Testing**:
   - Measure bundle size before and after cleanup
   - Compare application load times
   - Check for any improvements in Lighthouse scores

6. **Cross-Browser Testing**:
   - Test in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge
   - Verify no browser-specific issues were introduced

7. **Error Monitoring**:
   - Deploy to a staging environment
   - Monitor error logs for any new errors
   - Check for any increase in error rates

8. **Validation Process**:
   - For each removed component, document:
     - Component name and location
     - Verification method used to confirm it was unused
     - Any dependencies that were also removed
     - Impact on bundle size (if measurable)

9. **Final Verification Checklist**:
   - All tests pass
   - Build process completes successfully
   - All application features work as expected
   - No new errors in console
   - No visual regressions
   - Bundle size has decreased or remained the same
   - Application performance is the same or better

tasks.title: Add ts-reset to Improve TypeScript Type Definitions
tasks.description: Install and configure ts-reset to enhance TypeScript's built-in typings with stricter and more intuitive type definitions for common JavaScript APIs, improving type safety across the project.
tasks.details:
## Implementation Steps

1. **Install ts-reset Package**:
   ```bash
   npm install --save-dev @total-typescript/ts-reset
   ```

2. **Create or Update TypeScript Entry Point**:
   Create a new file at the root of your TypeScript source:
   ```typescript
   // src/types/reset.d.ts
   import '@total-typescript/ts-reset';
   ```

3. **Ensure TypeScript Configuration Includes the Reset File**:
   Update `tsconfig.json` to include the reset file:
   ```json
   {
     "compilerOptions": {
       // existing options...
     },
     "include": [
       "src/**/*.ts",
       "src/**/*.tsx",
       "src/types/reset.d.ts"  // Add this line
     ]
   }
   ```

4. **Verify TypeScript Configuration**:
   Ensure your `tsconfig.json` has the following settings to maximize the benefits of ts-reset:
   ```json
   {
     "compilerOptions": {
       "strict": true,
       "noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true,
       // other existing options...
     }
   }
   ```

5. **Update Existing Code to Leverage Improved Types**:
   - Review areas of the codebase that work with arrays, promises, and JSON parsing
   - Update code to take advantage of stricter types, particularly:
     - Array methods (`.find()`, `.filter()`, etc.)
     - `JSON.parse()` return types
     - Promise handling

6. **Document Type Improvements**:
   Create a brief documentation entry in the project README or developer documentation explaining:
   - What ts-reset is and why it's being used

   // Test array find returns properly typed

   const found = numbers.find(n => n === 4);

tasks.title: Implement Mobile Menu Functionality with Accessibility Support
tasks.description: Implement a responsive mobile menu with hamburger icon, slide-out navigation drawer, smooth animations, and full accessibility support including ARIA attributes and keyboard navigation.
tasks.testStrategy:
## Test Strategy

1. **Accessibility Testing**:
   - Use automated tools like Axe or Lighthouse to check for accessibility issues
   - Test with screen readers (VoiceOver on iOS/macOS, NVDA or JAWS on Windows)
   - Verify keyboard navigation works properly:
     - Menu can be opened with Enter/Space on the hamburger button
     - Tab navigation cycles through all menu items
     - Escape key closes the menu
     - Focus returns to the hamburger button when menu is closed
   - Verify proper ARIA attributes are present and correctly updated
   - Test with different color contrast settings and zoom levels

2. **Responsive Design Testing**:
   - Test on various screen sizes (320px, 375px, 414px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px)
   - Verify the mobile menu is hidden on screens ≥768px (md breakpoint)
   - Verify the desktop navigation is hidden on screens <768px
   - Test the transition between mobile and desktop layouts at the breakpoint
   - Ensure the menu is usable in both portrait and landscape orientations

3. **Interaction Testing**:
   - Verify the hamburger icon toggles the menu open/closed
   - Test that clicking outside the menu closes it
   - Verify that clicking a navigation link closes the menu and navigates to the correct page
   - Test that the back button works correctly after navigation
   - Verify that scrolling is locked when the menu is open
   - Test touch gestures (swipe to close) if implemented

4. **Animation Testing**:
   - Verify animations are smooth (60fps) on actual mobile devices
   - Test on low-end devices to ensure performance is acceptable
   - Verify animations respect user preferences (reduced motion settings)
   - Test that animations complete correctly and don't get stuck

5. **Cross-Browser Testing**:
   - Test on Chrome, Safari, Firefox, and Edge
   - Test on iOS Safari and Chrome for Android
   - Verify functionality on older browser versions if required by project requirements

6. **Integration Testing**:
   - Verify the mobile menu works correctly with the language switching functionality
   - Test that active page highlighting works correctly
   - Verify the menu state resets appropriately when navigating between pages
   - Test that the menu works correctly with dynamic content changes

7. **User Testing**:
   - Conduct usability testing with real users on actual mobile devices
   - Gather feedback on the menu's usability, accessibility, and performance
   - Make adjustments based on user feedback

tasks.title: Implement Comprehensive SEO Optimization
tasks.description: Optimize the DeskoHub website for search engines by implementing meta tags, structured data, sitemap generation, robots.txt, social media tags, canonical URLs, and performance improvements to enhance search visibility and rankings.
tasks.testStrategy:
## Test Strategy

1. **Meta Tags Validation**:
   - Use browser developer tools to inspect meta tags on each page
   - Verify that title, description, and keywords are correctly set
   - Check that dynamic metadata is generated correctly for different pages
   - Test that language-specific metadata is correctly applied based on locale

2. **Structured Data Testing**:
   ```bash
   # Use Google's Rich Results Test tool
   npx structured-data-testing-tool@latest https://deskohub.com
   ```
   - Validate JSON-LD implementation using Google's Rich Results Test tool
   - Check for errors or warnings in structured data implementation
   - Verify that Organization, LocalBusiness, and other schema types are correctly formatted

3. **Sitemap and Robots.txt Verification**:
   - Access `/sitemap.xml` and verify it contains all expected URLs
   - Check that dynamic pages are correctly included in the sitemap
   - Verify that `/robots.txt` has the correct rules and sitemap reference
   - Test that disallowed paths are properly specified

4. **Open Graph and Twitter Card Testing**:
   - Use Facebook's Sharing Debugger (https://developers.facebook.com/tools/debug/)
   - Test with Twitter Card Validator (https://cards-dev.twitter.com/validator)
   - Verify images load correctly and have appropriate dimensions
   - Check that titles and descriptions appear as expected in social previews

5. **Canonical URL Testing**:
   - Inspect page source to verify canonical URLs are correctly set
   - Test on multiple pages to ensure dynamic canonical URL generation works
   - Verify that language variants have appropriate canonical references

6. **Performance Testing for SEO**:
   ```bash
   # Run Lighthouse audit
   npx lighthouse https://deskohub.com --output=html --output-path=./lighthouse-report.html
   ```
   - Run Lighthouse audits to check SEO score (aim for 90+ score)
   - Verify Core Web Vitals metrics (LCP, FID, CLS) meet Google's requirements
   - Test page load times on mobile and desktop devices
   - Check image optimization implementation with WebPageTest

7. **Cross-Browser and Device Testing**:
   - Test SEO implementations across Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge
   - Verify mobile-friendly implementation using Google's Mobile-Friendly Test
   - Check responsive behavior of SEO elements on different screen sizes

8. **Internationalization SEO Testing**:
   - Verify hreflang tags are correctly implemented for language variants
   - Test language switching maintains proper SEO attributes
   - Check that search engines can correctly identify language-specific content

9. **Accessibility Impact Testing**:
   - Run accessibility audits to ensure SEO changes don't negatively impact accessibility
   - Verify screen readers can properly interpret structured data
   - Check that SEO optimizations maintain WCAG compliance

10. **Search Console Integration**:
    - Verify site is properly indexed in Google Search Console
    - Check for crawl errors or issues after implementation
    - Monitor performance metrics after deployment
    - Test URL inspection tool to ensure Google can properly crawl pages

11. **Regression Testing**:
    - Verify that SEO implementations don't break existing functionality
    - Check that page transitions and navigation still work correctly
    - Test that dynamic content loading still functions with SEO enhancements

tasks.title: Fix Security Vulnerability: Move DOTYPOS_CLIENT_SECRET to Server-Side Only
tasks.description: CANCELLED: This task was based on a misunderstanding. NEXT_PUBLIC_DOTYPOS_CLIENT_SECRET is intended to be a client-side secret for OAuth flow. This is by design and not a vulnerability.
tasks.details:
## Task Cancellation Reason

This task has been cancelled because it was based on a misunderstanding of the OAuth flow implementation:

1. **Not a Security Vulnerability**: The `NEXT_PUBLIC_DOTYPOS_CLIENT_SECRET` is intentionally designed to be used on the client-side as part of the OAuth authentication flow.

2. **By Design**: Exposing this particular client secret in the client-side code is part of the intended implementation for the Dotypos OAuth flow.

3. **No Action Required**: There is no need to move this secret to server-side only environment variables as originally planned.

## Original Misconception

The task was created based on the general security principle that secrets should not be exposed in client-side code. However, in this specific implementation with Dotypos, the client secret is meant to be used in the client-side OAuth flow.

## Next Steps

1. **Documentation Update**: Consider adding documentation that explains why this particular client secret is exposed on the client-side to prevent future confusion.

2. **Security Review**: While this specific case is by design, it may be worth conducting a general security review to identify any actual vulnerabilities in the authentication system.
tasks.testStrategy:
## Test Strategy

Since this task has been cancelled, no testing is required for implementation. However, the following verification steps may be useful:

1. **Confirm OAuth Flow Works As Expected**:
   - Verify that the current implementation with `NEXT_PUBLIC_DOTYPOS_CLIENT_SECRET` on the client-side functions correctly
   - Test the complete OAuth flow to ensure authentication works properly

2. **Documentation Check**:
   - Ensure any documentation about environment variables clearly indicates that `NEXT_PUBLIC_DOTYPOS_CLIENT_SECRET` is intentionally exposed on the client-side
   - Add comments in the code where the client secret is used to explain that this is by design

3. **Security Verification**:
   - Confirm with Dotypos documentation or support that this implementation follows their recommended OAuth flow
   - Verify that no other unintended secrets are exposed in the client-side code

tasks.title: Complete Board Game Data for Missing Information
tasks.description: Research and update 33 board games in the board-games-data.ts file that have missing duration and rating information, including games like Cristal Palace, Desítka, Ecologic, and others.
tasks.details:
## Objective
Update the board game data collection with accurate information for 33 games that currently have missing duration and BGG rating values.

## Implementation Steps

1. **Identify the Target Games**:
   - Review the board-games-data.ts file to confirm the 33 games with duration=0 and rating=0
   - Create a tracking spreadsheet or document to organize the research process

2. **Research Game Information**:
   - Use BoardGameGeek (BGG) as the primary source for ratings and duration information
   - For each game, search on BGG by exact name
   - If the exact name doesn't yield results, try alternative spellings or the original language name
   - For Czech-specific games that may not be on BGG, consult specialized Czech board game sites like zatrolene-hry.cz or deskofobie.cz

3. **Update the Data Structure**:
   ```typescript
   // Example of how to update the data in board-games-data.ts
   export const boardGames: BoardGame[] = [
     // Example of updating an existing entry
     {
       id: "cristal-palace",
       name: "Cristal Palace",
       nameEn: "Cristal Palace",
       // Update these values from 0 to actual data
       duration: 90, // Duration in minutes
       rating: 7.2, // BGG rating out of 10
       // Keep other properties unchanged
       minPlayers: 2,
       maxPlayers: 4,
       complexity: 3.2,
       // ...
     },
     // Repeat for all 33 games
   ];
   ```

4. **Handle Edge Cases**:
   - For games with variable play time, use the average or expected duration
   - For games with multiple editions, ensure you're using data for the correct edition
   - For games not found on BGG, document alternative sources used for the information
   - If a game truly has no BGG rating (very obscure or new), consider using a placeholder like -1 and add a comment

5. **Document Research Sources**:
   - Create a simple markdown document listing each game and the source of information
   - Include direct links to BGG pages or other authoritative sources
   - This will help with future updates and verification

6. **Update Type Definitions if Needed**:
   - Ensure the BoardGame type definition accommodates any special cases discovered
   - If changes to the type are needed, update the interface definition

7. **Verify Data Consistency**:
   - After updating all games, review the data for consistency in format and style
   - Ensure all durations are in minutes and all ratings use the same scale
tasks.testStrategy:
## Test Strategy

1. **Data Validation**:
   - Verify that all 33 previously missing entries now have non-zero values for duration and rating
   - Confirm that the data types are correct (numbers, not strings)
   - Check that ratings are within the expected range (typically 1-10 for BGG)
   - Ensure durations are reasonable for each game type

2. **Cross-Reference Verification**:
   - Randomly select 10 of the updated games and independently verify their information against BGG
   - Compare the entered values with the source data to ensure accuracy

3. **UI Testing**:
   - Check that the updated data displays correctly on the Board Games page
   - Verify that sorting and filtering features work properly with the new data
   - Confirm that game cards show the correct duration and rating information

4. **Edge Case Testing**:
   - Test any games that had special handling requirements
   - Verify that games with unusual duration formats (ranges, etc.) display appropriately
   - Check that any games still without ratings (if any) are handled gracefully in the UI

5. **Documentation Review**:
   - Review the research sources document for completeness
   - Verify that all 33 games have documented sources for their information

6. **Regression Testing**:
   - Ensure that the updates to these 33 games didn't affect the data for other games
   - Verify that all board game functionality continues to work as expected

tasks.title: Implement Pagination for Board Games List
tasks.description: Add pagination or "load more" functionality to the board games list to improve performance and user experience, as the current implementation displays 127+ games at once causing performance issues.
tasks.testStrategy:
## Test Strategy

1. **Functionality Testing**:
   - Verify pagination controls appear correctly when there are more than one page of games
   - Test navigation between pages using pagination controls
   - Confirm the correct number of games are displayed per page (24 recommended)
   - Verify URL parameters update correctly when changing pages
   - Test that the current page is visually highlighted in the pagination controls
   - If using "Load More" approach, verify more games load when clicking the button
   - Test that the "Load More" button disappears when all games are loaded

2. **Performance Testing**:
   - Measure and compare page load times before and after implementation
   - Use Chrome DevTools Performance tab to analyze rendering performance
   - Verify memory usage is reduced compared to loading all games at once
   - Test scrolling performance with paginated results vs. all games
   - Measure Time to Interactive (TTI) and First Contentful Paint (FCP) metrics

3. **User Experience Testing**:
   - Verify smooth scrolling to top when changing pages
   - Test loading states and ensure appropriate feedback is shown during page transitions
   - Confirm that the page maintains its position when navigating back to the games list
   - Test keyboard navigation through pagination controls
   - Verify focus management when changing pages

4. **Responsive Design Testing**:
   - Test pagination controls on various screen sizes (mobile, tablet, desktop)
   - Verify the number of visible page numbers adapts to screen width
   - Confirm touch interactions work properly on mobile devices
   - Test that the pagination UI remains usable on small screens

5. **Accessibility Testing**:
   - Verify all pagination controls have proper ARIA attributes
   - Test keyboard navigation through pagination controls
   - Ensure color contrast meets WCAG standards
   - Test with screen readers to confirm proper announcements when changing pages
   - Verify focus is managed correctly when navigating between pages

6. **Edge Case Testing**:
   - Test with very large number of games (1000+)
   - Test with exactly one page of games (pagination should not appear)
   - Test with zero games (appropriate empty state should be shown)
   - Test with different page sizes to ensure calculations work correctly
   - Verify behavior when directly accessing a URL with an invalid page parameter

7. **Integration Testing**:
   - Test pagination with category filtering to ensure they work together
   - Verify browser back/forward navigation works correctly with pagination
   - Test that game details modal/dialog still functions correctly with paginated results

tasks.testStrategy:
## Test Strategy

1. **Unit Testing for Template Service**:

   - Verify the template caching mechanism works correctly
   - Test error handling when a template file is missing or invalid
   - Verify the `getTemplateNameForEmailType` function returns the correct template name for each email type

2. **Visual Testing of Email Templates**:
   - Use email testing tools like Litmus or Email on Acid to preview templates in different email clients:
     - Gmail (web and mobile app)
     - Outlook (desktop and web)
     - Apple Mail
     - Yahoo Mail
     - Mobile email clients (iOS Mail, Gmail app)
   - Verify responsive design works correctly on different screen sizes
   - Check that all images display properly and have appropriate alt text

3. **Integration Testing**:
   - Test the complete email sending flow from user action to email delivery
   - Verify that the correct template is used for each email type
   - Test with real data from the application to ensure all variables are properly replaced
   - Verify that special characters and HTML entities are handled correctly

4. **Internationalization Testing**:
   - Test templates with both Czech and English content
   - Verify that date formats, currency symbols, and other locale-specific elements display correctly
   - Test with non-Latin characters to ensure proper encoding

5. **Accessibility Testing**:
   - Check color contrast for text readability
   - Ensure proper semantic structure for screen readers
   - Verify that links have descriptive text

6. **Manual Testing Checklist**:
   - Send test emails to multiple email addresses and verify receipt
   - Check that all links in the emails work correctly
   - Verify that the emails don't get flagged as spam
   - Test with different data lengths (e.g., very long names or messages) to ensure layout doesn't break

7. **Regression Testing**:
   - After implementing the new templates, verify that all existing email functionality continues to work
   - Check that no other parts of the application are affected by the template changes

tasks.title: Remove Debug Statements from Production Code
tasks.description: Remove all console.log and debug statements from production code or wrap them in development environment checks to prevent debug information from appearing in the production environment.
tasks.testStrategy:
## Test Strategy

1. **Static Code Analysis**:
   - Run ESLint with the updated configuration to verify no console statements remain:
   ```bash
   npx eslint --ext .ts,.tsx src/
   ```
   - Review any warnings or errors and address them

2. **Development Environment Testing**:
   - Start the application in development mode
   - Verify that wrapped debug statements still appear in the browser console
   - Check that the logger utility functions work as expected

3. **Production Build Testing**:
   - Create a production build:
   ```bash
   npm run build
   ```
   - Serve the production build locally:
   ```bash
   npm run start
   ```
   - Open browser developer tools and verify no debug statements appear in the console
   - Navigate through different parts of the application to ensure no debug logs appear

4. **Code Review**:
   - Have another developer review the changes to ensure all debug statements have been properly handled
   - Verify that the logger utility is implemented correctly

5. **Regression Testing**:
   - Verify that removing debug statements hasn't affected application functionality
   - Test key user flows to ensure everything still works as expected

6. **Documentation**:
   - Update the project documentation to include guidelines for logging in the application
   - Document the new logger utility and how it should be used for future development
